name: Raspberry Almond Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the filling by combining about 5 ounces almond paste with 2 tablespoons (1 stick) butter, softened to room temperature, 3/4 cup granulated sugar, 2 large eggs, 1 teaspoon baking powder, 1/2 teaspoon baking soda, 1 teaspoon almond extract, and 2 cups all-purpose flour.
2. In a separate bowl, mix together 1 cup plain yogurt or buttermilk with the remaining ingredients until well combined.
3. Line muffin pans with foil baking cups.
4. Cut the almond paste into 16 pieces and pat each piece into a round disk about 1 1/2 inches across.
5. Pour the batter evenly into each muffin cup, covering about 2 tablespoons of the mixture in the center.
6. Place a level teaspoon of raspberry preserves on top of each muffin, followed by a piece of almond paste.
7. Add another 2 tablespoons of batter to cover the preserves and almond paste.
8. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 25 to 30 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack for at least 10 minutes before serving.
